result:
Done. The change request is implemented, tested, and committed.

## What I found

The checkout was effectively empty — only `README.md`, `.gitignore`, and an untracked `package.json` (which defines the toolchain: no dependencies, `node --test`, `node --check`). Despite the "change request" framing, there was no existing app to modify, so I built a self-contained, dependency-free Node HTTP app that satisfies the requirements.

## What I built

- **`src/store.js`** — in-memory users + sessions, seeded with one admin and two members. Crucially, `userForToken` reads the role **live** from the user record on every call.
- **`src/auth.js`** — scrypt password hashing + random session tokens.
- **`src/app.js`** — the router:
  - `POST /sessions` → issues a bearer token.
  - `GET /users` (admin only) → `{users: [{id, name, email, role}]}`, no password hashes leaked.
  - `PATCH /users/:id/role` (admin only) → validates role ∈ {admin, member} (`400`), unknown user (`404`), and the **last-admin guard** (`409`).
- **`src/server.js`** — entry point; `test/role-management.test.js` — 11 tests.

## Requirements coverage

- **Admin-only** on both endpoints — verified members get `403`, anonymous get `401`.
- **Immediate effect for signed-in users** — because authorization resolves the role live per request, not from cached session state. Two tests prove it: a promoted member gains access on their existing token, and a demoted admin loses it instantly.
- **Last admin cannot be demoted** — `409`, verified.

All 11 tests pass; `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` are clean. Committed as `8c1ce6e`.

One design note worth flagging: I had to introduce the authentication/session layer myself (`POST /sessions`) since none existed — the task presupposed one. If your real system already has an auth mechanism, the role-check logic in `src/app.js` (`currentUser.role !== "admin"`) is what you'd graft onto it.
